citation.description: Line 19 contains 'IN-PLAN ASSETS ONLY: a finding must name the plan section/step that CREATES, MODIFIES, or TRANSMITS the asset at issue; an asset the plan does not touch (an existing credential, service, or store that is merely adjacent...) is OUT OF SCOPE and must not be a finding' — satisfying both oracle patterns 'in-plan' and 'does not touch'.

criteria.criterion: plan_review_T5c.md gains an explicit in-plan-asset scope rule naming the plan section/step; assets the plan does not touch are out of scope. Oracle: grep -ni "does not touch\|in-plan" returns at least one hit.

citation.description: Line 19 contains 'SAME-ROUND DEDUP: emit ONE finding per (surface, defect) pair; when several dimensions above implicate the same surface and defect, fold the additional angles into that one finding's rationale rather than emitting them as separate findings.' — 'ONE finding per' matches the oracle case-insensitively.

criteria.criterion: The prompt gains a same-round dedup rule: one finding per (surface, defect) pair; additional angles folded into that finding, not emitted separately. Oracle: grep -ni "one finding per" returns a hit.

citation.description: plan_review_T5c.md has category: 'plan-review-criterion' (not 'review'), so build_prompt_index() (prompts.py:273) skips it — the index is not affected by this file change and regeneration is not required. The 'if the build requires it' condition is not triggered.

criteria.criterion: The derived prompt index is regenerated if the build requires it and the CI prompt-drift gate passes.
